#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\
\"\"\"parse_document_pipeline.py\n\
\n\
Full PDF → Supabase pipeline:\n\
  1. Upload PDF to project-documents bucket\n\
  2. Insert project_documents row\n\
  3. Submit to doc-parser /parse/docling (passes SB creds so doc-parser uploads images)\n\
  4. Poll /status until complete\n\
  5. Fetch /results\n\
  6. Insert document_pages (one per page, with storage_url, thumbnail_url, category)\n\
  7. Insert document_images (floorplans, site_photos, diagrams)\n\
  8. Insert ocr_results (one per table, with markdown + structured_data)\n\
\n\
Usage:\n\
  python parse_document_pipeline.py \\\\\n\
    --pdf path/to/file.pdf \\\\\n\
    --tenant my_tenant \\\\\n\
    --project my_project \\\\\n\
    [--supabase-url http://host.docker.internal:54321] \\\\\n\
    [--supabase-key <service_role_key>] \\\\\n\
    [--api-url http://localhost:5000]\n\
\n\
Env vars (fallbacks):\n\
  SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, DOC_PARSER_URL\n\
\"\"\"\n\
\n\
import argparse\n\
import json\n\
import os\n\
import sys\n\
import time\n\
import uuid\n\
from pathlib import Path\n\
from dotenv import load_dotenv\n\
\n\
import requests\n\
from supabase import create_client, Client\n\
\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
# Load .env from doc-parser directory (same folder as this script)\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
_here = Path(__file__).parent\n\
load_dotenv(_here / \".env\")\n\
\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
# CLI args\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
\n\
def parse_args():\n\
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"PDF → Supabase parse pipeline\")\n\
    p.add_argument(\"--pdf\", required=True, help=\"Path to PDF file\")\n\
    p.add_argument(\"--tenant\", required=True, help=\"Tenant name (used for storage paths)\")\n\
    p.add_argument(\"--project\", required=True, help=\"Project name (used for storage paths)\")\n\
    p.add_argument(\n\
        \"--supabase-url\",\n\
        default=os.getenv(\"SUPABASE_URL\", \"http://host.docker.internal:54321\"),\n\
        help=\"Supabase API URL\",\n\
    )\n\
    p.add_argument(\n\
        \"--supabase-key\",\n\
        default=os.getenv(\"SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY\", \"\"),\n\
        help=\"Supabase service role key (required for storage uploads)\",\n\
    )\n\
    p.add_argument(\n\
        \"--api-url\",\n\
        default=os.getenv(\"DOC_PARSER_URL\", \"http://localhost:5000\"),\n\
        help=\"Doc-parser API base URL\",\n\
    )\n\
    p.add_argument(\n\
        \"--poll-interval\", type=float, default=3.0,\n\
        help=\"Seconds between status polls (default: 3)\",\n\
    )\n\
    return p.parse_args()\n\
\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
# Helpers\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
\n\
def log(msg: str):\n\
    print(f\"[pipeline] {msg}\", flush=True)\n\
\n\
\n\
def determine_category(page_data: dict) -> str:\n\
    \"\"\"Derive dominant page category from doc-parser page result.\"\"\"\n\
    fp = len(page_data.get(\"floorplans\", []))\n\
    tb = len(page_data.get(\"tables\", []))\n\
    sp = len(page_data.get(\"site_photos\", []))\n\
    dg = len(page_data.get(\"diagrams\", []))\n\
    total_images = fp + sp + dg\n\
    total = total_images + tb\n\
    if total == 0:\n\
        return \"text\"\n\
    if total_images > 0 and tb > 0:\n\
        return \"mixed\"\n\
    if fp > 0 and fp >= sp and fp >= dg:\n\
        return \"floorplan\"\n\
    if sp > 0 and sp >= fp and sp >= dg:\n\
        return \"site_photo\"\n\
    if dg > 0:\n\
        return \"diagram\"\n\
    if tb > 0:\n\
        return \"table\"\n\
    return \"text\"\n\
\n\
\n\
def parse_markdown_table(markdown: str) -> dict:\n\
    \"\"\"Convert markdown table string to {headers, rows} dict.\"\"\"\n\
    lines = [l.strip() for l in markdown.strip().splitlines() if l.strip()]\n\
    table_lines = [l for l in lines if \"|\" in l]\n\
    if not table_lines:\n\
        return {\"headers\": [], \"rows\": [], \"raw\": markdown}\n\
\n\
    def split_row(line):\n\
        return [c.strip() for c in line.strip(\"|\" ).split(\"|\")]\n\
\n\
    headers = split_row(table_lines[0]) if table_lines else []\n\
    rows = []\n\
    for line in table_lines[1:]:\n\
        # Skip separator rows like |---|---|---|\n\
        if all(c.strip(\"-: \") == \"\" for c in split_row(line)):\n\
            continue\n\
        rows.append(split_row(line))\n\
\n\
    filled = sum(1 for r in rows for c in r if c)\n\
    total_cells = len(rows) * len(headers) if headers and rows else 1\n\
    confidence = round(filled / total_cells, 3) if total_cells else 0.0\n\
\n\
    return {\"headers\": headers, \"rows\": rows, \"confidence\": confidence}\n\
\n\
\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
# Phase 1: Upload PDF + insert project_documents\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
\n\
def upload_pdf(sb: Client, pdf_path: Path, tenant: str, project: str) -> tuple[str, str]:\n\
    \"\"\"Upload PDF to storage, return (document_id, file_url).\"\"\"\n\
    storage_path = f\"{tenant}/{project}/{pdf_path.name}\"\n\
    log(f\"Uploading PDF to project-documents/{storage_path} ...\")\n\
\n\
    with open(pdf_path, \"rb\") as f:\n\
        data = f.read()\n\
\n\
    try:\n\
        sb.storage.from_(\"project-documents\").upload(\n\
            path=storage_path,\n\
            file=data,\n\
            file_options={\"content-type\": \"application/pdf\", \"upsert\": \"true\"},\n\
        )\n\
    except Exception as e:\n\
        log(f\"Storage upload error (may already exist): {e}\")\n\
\n\
    # Build public-ish URL (signed or path-based)\n\
    file_url = f\"{sb.storage_url}/object/project-documents/{storage_path}\"\n\
\n\
    # Insert project_documents row\n\
    doc_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\
    result = sb.table(\"project_documents\").insert({\n\
        \"id\": doc_id,\n\
        \"title\": pdf_path.stem,\n\
        \"file_url\": file_url,\n\
        \"file_type\": \"application/pdf\",\n\
        \"file_size_bytes\": pdf_path.stat().st_size,\n\
        \"metadata\": {\"tenant\": tenant, \"project\": project, \"storage_path\": storage_path},\n\
    }).execute()\n\
\n\
    if hasattr(result, \"error\") and result.error:\n\
        raise RuntimeError(f\"project_documents insert failed: {result.error}\")\n\
\n\
    log(f\"project_documents row created: {doc_id}\")\n\
    return doc_id, file_url\n\
\n\
\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
# Phase 2: Submit to doc-parser + poll\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
\n\
def submit_and_poll(api_url: str, pdf_path: Path, tenant: str, project: str,\n\
                    sb_url: str, sb_key: str, poll_interval: float) -> dict:\n\
    \"\"\"Submit PDF to /parse/docling, poll until done, return results.\"\"\"\n\
    log(\"Submitting to doc-parser /parse/docling ...\")\n\
\n\
    with open(pdf_path, \"rb\") as f:\n\
        resp = requests.post(\n\
            f\"{api_url}/parse/docling\",\n\
            files={\"file\": (pdf_path.name, f, \"application/pdf\")},\n\
            data={\n\
                \"tenant_name\": tenant,\n\
                \"project_name\": project,\n\
                \"supabase_url\": sb_url,\n\
                \"supabase_key\": sb_key,\n\
            },\n\
            timeout=30,\n\
        )\n\
    resp.raise_for_status()\n\
    job_id = resp.json()[\"job_id\"]\n\
    log(f\"Job submitted: {job_id}\")\n\
\n\
    # Poll\n\
    while True:\n\
        time.sleep(poll_interval)\n\
        status_resp = requests.get(f\"{api_url}/status/{job_id}\", timeout=10)\n\
        status_resp.raise_for_status()\n\
        status = status_resp.json()\n\
        pct = status.get(\"progress_pct\", 0)\n\
        step = status.get(\"current_step\", \"\")\n\
        state = status.get(\"status\", \"\")\n\
        log(f\"  [{state}] {pct:.0f}% — {step}\")\n\
        if state == \"complete\":\n\
            break\n\
        if state == \"error\":\n\
            raise RuntimeError(f\"Doc-parser job failed: {status.get('error')}\")\n\
\n\
    log(\"Fetching results ...\")\n\
    results_resp = requests.get(f\"{api_url}/results/{job_id}\", timeout=30)\n\
    results_resp.raise_for_status()\n\
    return results_resp.json()\n\
\n\
\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
# Phase 3+: Persist pages, images, OCR\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
\n\
def persist_results(sb: Client, document_id: str, results: dict):\n\
    pages_result = results.get(\"pages\", {})\n\
    total_pages = results.get(\"summary\", {}).get(\"pages_count\", len(pages_result))\n\
    log(f\"Persisting {total_pages} pages ...\")\n\
\n\
    page_id_map = {}  # page_number -> document_pages.id\n\
\n\
    # ── document_pages ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\
    for page_no_key, page_data in pages_result.items():\n\
        page_no = int(page_no_key)\n\
        category = determine_category(page_data)\n\
        page_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\
\n\
        sb.table(\"document_pages\").insert({\n\
            \"id\": page_id,\n\
            \"document_id\": document_id,\n\
            \"page_number\": page_no,\n\
            \"storage_url\": page_data.get(\"page_image_url\"),\n\
            \"thumbnail_url\": page_data.get(\"thumbnail_url\"),\n\
            \"category\": category,\n\
            \"metadata\": {\n\
                \"floorplans\": len(page_data.get(\"floorplans\", [])),\n\
                \"tables\": len(page_data.get(\"tables\", [])),\n\
                \"site_photos\": len(page_data.get(\"site_photos\", [])),\n\
                \"diagrams\": len(page_data.get(\"diagrams\", [])),\n\
            },\n\
        }).execute()\n\
\n\
        page_id_map[page_no] = page_id\n\
        log(f\"  Page {page_no}: category={category}, id={page_id}\")\n\
\n\
    # ── document_images ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\
    image_count = 0\n\
    for page_no_key, page_data in pages_result.items():\n\
        page_no = int(page_no_key)\n\
\n\
        # Floorplans (dicts with image_url + LLM data)\n\
        for fp in page_data.get(\"floorplans\", []):\n\
            if \"error\" in fp:\n\
                continue\n\
            img_url = fp.get(\"image_url\")\n\
            if not img_url:\n\
                continue\n\
            sb.table(\"document_images\").insert({\n\
                \"document_id\": document_id,\n\
                \"image_url\": img_url,\n\
                \"page_number\": page_no,\n\
                \"image_type\": \"floorplan\",\n\
                \"caption\": fp.get(\"general_notes\", [None])[0] if fp.get(\"general_notes\") else None,\n\
                \"metadata\": {k: v for k, v in fp.items() if k != \"image_url\"},\n\
            }).execute()\n\
            image_count += 1\n\
\n\
        # Site photos\n\
        for sp in page_data.get(\"site_photos\", []):\n\
            img_url = sp.get(\"image_url\") if isinstance(sp, dict) else None\n\
            if not img_url:\n\
                continue\n\
            sb.table(\"document_images\").insert({\n\
                \"document_id\": document_id,\n\
                \"image_url\": img_url,\n\
                \"page_number\": page_no,\n\
                \"image_type\": \"site_photo\",\n\
                \"metadata\": {},\n\
            }).execute()\n\
            image_count += 1\n\
\n\
        # Diagrams\n\
        for dg in page_data.get(\"diagrams\", []):\n\
            img_url = dg.get(\"image_url\") if isinstance(dg, dict) else None\n\
            if not img_url:\n\
                continue\n\
            sb.table(\"document_images\").insert({\n\
                \"document_id\": document_id,\n\
                \"image_url\": img_url,\n\
                \"page_number\": page_no,\n\
                \"image_type\": \"diagram\",\n\
                \"metadata\": {},\n\
            }).execute()\n\
            image_count += 1\n\
\n\
    log(f\"  Inserted {image_count} document_images rows\")\n\
\n\
    # ── ocr_results ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\
    ocr_count = 0\n\
    for page_no_key, page_data in pages_result.items():\n\
        page_no = int(page_no_key)\n\
        page_id = page_id_map.get(page_no)\n\
\n\
        for tbl_idx, markdown in enumerate(page_data.get(\"tables\", [])):\n\
            parsed = parse_markdown_table(markdown)\n\
            confidence = parsed.pop(\"confidence\", None)\n\
            sb.table(\"ocr_results\").insert({\n\
                \"document_id\": document_id,\n\
                \"page_id\": page_id,\n\
                \"table_index\": tbl_idx,\n\
                \"markdown\": markdown,\n\
                \"structured_data\": parsed,\n\
                \"confidence\": confidence,\n\
            }).execute()\n\
            ocr_count += 1\n\
\n\
    log(f\"  Inserted {ocr_count} ocr_results rows\")\n\
\n\
\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
# Main\n\
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\
\n\
def main():\n\
    args = parse_args()\n\
\n\
    pdf_path = Path(args.pdf).resolve()\n\
    if not pdf_path.exists():\n\
        log(f\"ERROR: PDF not found: {pdf_path}\")\n\
        sys.exit(1)\n\
\n\
    if not args.supabase_key:\n\
        log(\"ERROR: --supabase-key is required (or set SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY).\")\n\
        log(\"  Run `supabase status` on Windows to get the service_role_key.\")\n\
        sys.exit(1)\n\
\n\
    log(f\"PDF:          {pdf_path}\")\n\
    log(f\"Tenant:       {args.tenant}\")\n\
    log(f\"Project:      {args.project}\")\n\
    log(f\"Supabase URL: {args.supabase_url}\")\n\
    log(f\"API URL:      {args.api_url}\")\n\
\n\
    sb: Client = create_client(args.supabase_url, args.supabase_key)\n\
\n\
    # Phase 1\n\
    document_id, file_url = upload_pdf(sb, pdf_path, args.tenant, args.project)\n\
\n\
    # Phase 2\n\
    results = submit_and_poll(\n\
        args.api_url, pdf_path, args.tenant, args.project,\n\
        args.supabase_url, args.supabase_key, args.poll_interval,\n\
    )\n\
\n\
    # Save raw results alongside PDF for debugging\n\
    results_path = pdf_path.parent / f\"{pdf_path.stem}_pipeline_results.json\"\n\
    results_path.write_text(json.dumps(results, indent=2))\n\
    log(f\"Raw results saved to {results_path}\")\n\
\n\
    # Phases 3-5\n\
    persist_results(sb, document_id, results)\n\
\n\
    log(\"\")\n\
    log(f\"Done. document_id = {document_id}\")\n\
    log(f\"Summary: {json.dumps(results.get('summary', {}), indent=2)}\")\n\
\n\
\n\
if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n\
    main()\n\

 * Doc-Parser Proxy Routes for Hono API
 *
 * Integrates doc-parser service into the existing work-orders API.
 * Provides engine selection, status polling, and result retrieval.
 *
 * Routes:
 *   POST /api/doc-parser/parse           - Parse document (engine selection)
 *   GET  /api/doc-parser/status/:jobId  - Lightweight status (poll-safe)
 *   GET  /api/doc-parser/results/:jobId - Full results (fetch-once)
 *   GET  /api/doc-parser/health         - Service health check
 */

import { Hono } from 'hono';
import { getScopedClient } from '../middleware/supabase';
import {
  submitParseJob,
  getJobStatus,
  getJobResults,
  checkHealth,
  mapToParseDocument,
  type DocParserStatus,
  type DocParserResult,
} from '../services/doc-parser-client';

const docParserRoutes = new Hono();

// ============================================================
// POST /parse
// Accepts JSON { document_id, engine }
// Fetches PDF from Supabase storage, forwards to doc-parser
// ============================================================

docParserRoutes.post('/parse', async (c) => {
  const body = await c.req.json().catch(() => ({}));
  const documentId = body?.document_id;
  const engine = (body?.engine || 'hybrid') as string;

  if (!documentId) {
    return c.json({ error: 'document_id is required' }, 400);
  }

  // ---- Claude Direct: existing flow (unchanged) ----
  if (engine === 'claude-direct') {
    return c.json({
      error: 'claude-direct engine should be handled by existing route handler',
      hint: 'This route only handles docling and hybrid engines',
    }, 400);
  }

  // ---- Docling or Hybrid: proxy to doc-parser service ----
  if (engine !== 'docling' && engine !== 'hybrid') {
    return c.json(
      { error: `Unknown engine: ${engine}. Use 'claude-direct', 'docling', or 'hybrid'.` },
      400,
    );
  }

  try {
    // Fetch document metadata from database
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const { data: document, error: fetchError } = await supabase
      .from('project_documents')
      .select('*')
      .eq('id', documentId)
      .single();

    if (fetchError || !document) {
      return c.json({ error: 'Document not found' }, 404);
    }

    if (!document.file_type?.includes('pdf')) {
      return c.json({ error: 'Document is not a PDF' }, 400);
    }

    // Download PDF from Supabase storage
    const { data: fileData, error: downloadError } = await supabase.storage
      .from('project-documents')
      .download(document.file_url);

    if (downloadError || !fileData) {
      console.error('[doc-parser] Storage download failed:', downloadError);
      return c.json({ error: 'Failed to download document from storage' }, 500);
    }

    const pdfBuffer = await fileData.arrayBuffer();

    // Submit to doc-parser service
    const jobResponse = await submitParseJob(
      pdfBuffer,
      document.title || `document-${documentId}.pdf`,
      engine as 'docling' | 'hybrid',
      {
        provider: body?.provider || process.env.DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER,
        tenantName: body?.tenant_name,
        projectName: body?.project_name || documentId,
        supabaseUrl: process.env.SUPABASE_URL,
        supabaseKey: process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY,
      },
    );

    // Return job info with polling URLs
    return c.json({
      engine,
      job_id: jobResponse.job_id,
      status: jobResponse.status,
      poll_url: `/api/doc-parser/status/${jobResponse.job_id}`,
      results_url: `/api/doc-parser/results/${jobResponse.job_id}`,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error';
    console.error(`[doc-parser] Parse submission failed: ${message}`);
    return c.json({ error: `Doc-parser submission failed: ${message}` }, 502);
  }
});

// ============================================================
// GET /api/doc-parser/status/:jobId
// Lightweight status for polling (Priority 4a)
// Safe to call every 2-5 seconds. Small payload.
// ============================================================

docParserRoutes.get('/status/:jobId', async (c) => {
  const jobId = c.req.param('jobId');

  try {
    const status: DocParserStatus = await getJobStatus(jobId);

    // Ensure result data is NEVER included in status response
    // (defense-in-depth - server should already exclude it)
    const safeStatus = { ...status };
    delete (safeStatus as any).result;

    return c.json(safeStatus);
  } catch (error) {
    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error';
    if (message.includes('not found')) {
      return c.json({ error: `Job ${jobId} not found` }, 404);
    }
    return c.json({ error: `Status check failed: ${message}` }, 502);
  }
});

// ============================================================
// GET /api/doc-parser/results/:jobId
// Full results - fetch once after status confirms "completed"
// Heavy payload. Includes ParsedDocument-compatible mapping.
// ============================================================

docParserRoutes.get('/results/:jobId', async (c) => {
  const jobId = c.req.param('jobId');
  const summaryOnly = c.req.query('summary') === 'true';

  try {
    const data = await getJobResults(jobId, summaryOnly);

    // If summary requested, return as-is
    if (summaryOnly) {
      return c.json(data);
    }

    // If hybrid engine with units array, map to ParsedDocument format
    const result = data.result as DocParserResult | undefined;
    if (result?.engine === 'hybrid' && result?.units) {
      return c.json({
        ...data,
        parsed: mapToParseDocument(result, jobId),
      });
    }

    // Docling engine - also map units if available
    if (result?.units) {
      return c.json({
        ...data,
        parsed: mapToParseDocument(result, jobId),
      });
    }

    // Fallback: return raw result
    return c.json(data);
  } catch (error) {
    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error';
    if (message.includes('not found')) {
      return c.json({ error: `Results for job ${jobId} not found` }, 404);
    }
    return c.json({ error: `Results fetch failed: ${message}` }, 502);
  }
});

// ============================================================
// GET /api/doc-parser/health
// Service health check with dependency status
// ============================================================

docParserRoutes.get('/health', async (c) => {
  const health = await checkHealth();
  const statusCode = health.status === 'healthy' ? 200 : 503;
  return c.json(health, statusCode);
});

export { docParserRoutes };
export default docParserRoutes;

import { OpenAPIHono } from '@hono/zod-openapi';
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
import { SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY } from '@repo/config';
import { PDFParser } from '../services/pdfParser';
import { ClaudePdfParser, generateWorkOrdersFromParsedData } from '../services/claudePdfParser';
import * as canvas from '@napi-rs/canvas';

// Maximum dimension for Claude multi-image API
const MAX_IMAGE_DIMENSION = 2000;

/**
 * Resize an image to fit within MAX_IMAGE_DIMENSION while maintaining aspect ratio
 */
async function resizeImageIfNeeded(
    base64Data: string,
    mediaType: 'image/png' | 'image/jpeg'
): Promise<{ data: string; mediaType: 'image/png' | 'image/jpeg'; resized: boolean }> {
    // Decode base64 to buffer
    const buffer = Buffer.from(base64Data, 'base64');

    // Load image
    const image = await canvas.loadImage(buffer);
    const width = image.width;
    const height = image.height;

    // Check if resizing is needed
    if (width <= MAX_IMAGE_DIMENSION && height <= MAX_IMAGE_DIMENSION) {
        return { data: base64Data, mediaType, resized: false };
    }

    // Calculate new dimensions maintaining aspect ratio
    let newWidth: number;
    let newHeight: number;

    if (width > height) {
        newWidth = MAX_IMAGE_DIMENSION;
        newHeight = Math.round((height / width) * MAX_IMAGE_DIMENSION);
    } else {
        newHeight = MAX_IMAGE_DIMENSION;
        newWidth = Math.round((width / height) * MAX_IMAGE_DIMENSION);
    }

    // Create canvas and draw resized image
    const resizedCanvas = canvas.createCanvas(newWidth, newHeight);
    const ctx = resizedCanvas.getContext('2d');
    ctx.drawImage(image, 0, 0, newWidth, newHeight);

    // Encode back to base64
    let outputBuffer: Buffer;
    if (mediaType === 'image/png') {
        outputBuffer = resizedCanvas.toBuffer('image/png');
    } else {
        outputBuffer = resizedCanvas.toBuffer('image/jpeg');
    }

    const newBase64 = outputBuffer.toString('base64');

    console.log(`Resized image from ${width}x${height} to ${newWidth}x${newHeight}`);

    return { data: newBase64, mediaType, resized: true };
}

export const documentsRoute = new OpenAPIHono();

// CREATE SCOPED CLIENT HELPER
const getScopedClient = (c: any) => {
    const token = c.req.header('Authorization')?.replace('Bearer ', '');
    if (token) {
        return createClient(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, {
            global: { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } }
        });
    }
    // For testing without auth - use service role to bypass RLS
    return createClient(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY);
};

// ============================================================================
// PROJECT DOCUMENTS ENDPOINTS
// ============================================================================

// LIST DOCUMENTS FOR A PROJECT
documentsRoute.get('/projects/:projectId/documents', async (c) => {
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const projectId = c.req.param('projectId');

    const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('project_documents')
        .select('*')
        .eq('project_id', projectId)
        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });

    if (error) {
        return c.json({ error: error.message }, 500);
    }

    return c.json(data);
});

// UPLOAD DOCUMENT TO PROJECT
documentsRoute.post('/projects/:projectId/documents', async (c) => {
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const projectId = c.req.param('projectId');

    try {
        // Get form data
        const formData = await c.req.formData();
        const file = formData.get('file') as File;
        const title = formData.get('title') as string || file.name;
        const description = formData.get('description') as string || '';

        if (!file) {
            return c.json({ error: 'No file provided' }, 400);
        }

        // Convert File to Buffer
        const arrayBuffer = await file.arrayBuffer();
        const buffer = Buffer.from(arrayBuffer);

        // Generate unique filename
        const timestamp = Date.now();
        const filename = `${projectId}/${timestamp}_${file.name}`;

        // Upload to Supabase Storage
        const { data: uploadData, error: uploadError } = await supabase.storage
            .from('project-documents')
            .upload(filename, buffer, {
                contentType: file.type,
                upsert: false
            });

        if (uploadError) {
            console.error('Upload error:', uploadError);
            return c.json({ error: uploadError.message }, 500);
        }

        // Get file metadata
        const metadata: any = {
            original_name: file.name,
            mime_type: file.type
        };

        // For PDFs, try to get page count
        if (file.type === 'application/pdf') {
            try {
                const parser = new PDFParser(supabase);
                const textData = await parser.extractText(buffer);
                metadata.page_count = textData.pages.length;
            } catch (error) {
                console.error('Failed to extract PDF metadata:', error);
            }
        }

        // Create document record
        const { data: document, error: dbError } = await supabase
            .from('project_documents')
            .insert({
                project_id: projectId,
                title,
                description,
                file_url: uploadData.path,
                file_type: file.type,
                file_size_bytes: file.size,
                metadata
            })
            .select()
            .single();

        if (dbError) {
            console.error('Database error:', dbError);
            // Try to clean up uploaded file
            await supabase.storage.from('project-documents').remove([uploadData.path]);
            return c.json({ error: dbError.message }, 500);
        }

        return c.json(document, 201);
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Error uploading document:', error);
        return c.json({
            error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to upload document'
        }, 500);
    }
});

// DELETE DOCUMENT
documentsRoute.delete('/documents/:id', async (c) => {
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const id = c.req.param('id');

    // Get document to find file path
    const { data: document, error: fetchError } = await supabase
        .from('project_documents')
        .select('file_url')
        .eq('id', id)
        .single();

    if (fetchError || !document) {
        return c.json({ error: 'Document not found' }, 404);
    }

    // Delete from storage
    const { error: storageError } = await supabase.storage
        .from('project-documents')
        .remove([document.file_url]);

    if (storageError) {
        console.error('Storage deletion error:', storageError);
    }

    // Delete associated images from storage
    const { data: images } = await supabase
        .from('document_images')
        .select('image_url')
        .eq('document_id', id);

    if (images && images.length > 0) {
        const imageUrls = images.map(img => img.image_url);
        const { error: imagesStorageError } = await supabase.storage
            .from('project-documents')
            .remove(imageUrls);
            
        if (imagesStorageError) {
            console.error('Associated images storage deletion error:', imagesStorageError);
        }
    }

    // Delete from database (cascade will handle related records)
    const { error: dbError } = await supabase
        .from('project_documents')
        .delete()
        .eq('id', id);

    if (dbError) {
        return c.json({ error: dbError.message }, 500);
    }

    return c.json({ success: true });
});

// DOWNLOAD DOCUMENT
documentsRoute.get('/documents/:id/download', async (c) => {
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const id = c.req.param('id');

    // Get document
    const { data: document, error: fetchError } = await supabase
        .from('project_documents')
        .select('*')
        .eq('id', id)
        .single();

    if (fetchError || !document) {
        return c.json({ error: 'Document not found' }, 404);
    }

    // Create signed URL for download
    const { data: signedData, error: urlError } = await supabase.storage
        .from('project-documents')
        .createSignedUrl(document.file_url, 3600); // 1 hour expiry

    if (urlError) {
        return c.json({ error: urlError.message }, 500);
    }

    return c.json({
        url: signedData.signedUrl,
        filename: document.title,
        expires_at: new Date(Date.now() + 3600000).toISOString()
    });
});

documentsRoute.post('/documents/:id/extract-images', async (c) => {

        // Get document
        const { data: document, error: fetchError } = await supabase
            .from('project_documents')
            .select('*')
            .eq('id', id)
            .single();

        if (fetchError || !document) {
            return c.json({ error: 'Document not found' }, 404);
        }

        // Check if it's a PDF
        if (!document.file_type.includes('pdf')) {
            return c.json({ error: 'Document is not a PDF' }, 400);

        if (downloadError || !fileData) {

            buffer,
            document.project_id,
            id
        );

        return c.json({
            success: true,
            images_extracted: images.length,
            images
        });
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Error extracting images:', error);
        return c.json({
            error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to extract images'
        }, 500);
    }
});

// PARSE DOCUMENT WITH CLAUDE
documentsRoute.post('/documents/:id/parse', async (c) => {
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const id = c.req.param('id');

    try {
        // Check for API key
        const apiKey = process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY;
        if (!apiKey) {
            return c.json({ error: 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not configured' }, 500);
        }

        // Get request body for optional custom prompt
        let customPrompt: string | undefined;
        try {
            const body = await c.req.json();
            customPrompt = body?.customPrompt;
        } catch {
            // No body provided, use default prompt
        }

        // Get document
        const { data: document, error: fetchError } = await supabase
            .from('project_documents')
            .select('*')
            .eq('id', id)
            .single();

        if (fetchError || !document) {
            return c.json({ error: 'Document not found' }, 404);
        }

        // Get extracted images for this document
        const { data: images, error: imagesError } = await supabase
            .from('document_images')
            .select('*')
            .eq('document_id', id)
            .order('page_number', { ascending: true });

        if (imagesError) {
            return c.json({ error: 'Failed to fetch document images' }, 500);
        }

        if (!images || images.length === 0) {
            return c.json({
                error: 'No images found. Please extract images first using /documents/:id/extract-images'
            }, 400);
        }

        console.log(`Parsing document ${id} with ${images.length} images using Claude...`);

        // Download images, resize if needed, and convert to base64
        // (Claude API requires HTTPS URLs but local Supabase uses HTTP, so we send base64 data)
        // Claude also limits images to 2000px max dimension for multi-image requests
        const base64Images: { data: string; mediaType: 'image/png' | 'image/jpeg' }[] = [];

        for (const image of images) {
            try {
                // Download image from storage
                const { data: imageData, error: downloadError } = await supabase.storage
                    .from('project-documents')
                    .download(image.image_url);

                if (downloadError || !imageData) {
                    console.error(`Failed to download image ${image.id}:`, downloadError);
                    continue;
                }

                // Convert to base64
                const arrayBuffer = await imageData.arrayBuffer();
                const base64 = Buffer.from(arrayBuffer).toString('base64');
                const mediaType = image.image_url.endsWith('.png') ? 'image/png' : 'image/jpeg';

                // Resize if needed (Claude limits images to 2000px for multi-image requests)
                const resizedImage = await resizeImageIfNeeded(base64, mediaType);

                base64Images.push({ data: resizedImage.data, mediaType: resizedImage.mediaType });
                const sizeKB = (Buffer.from(resizedImage.data, 'base64').length / 1024).toFixed(1);
                console.log(`Loaded image ${image.page_number || 'unknown'} (${sizeKB} KB)${resizedImage.resized ? ' [resized]' : ''}`);
            } catch (err) {
                console.error(`Failed to process image ${image.id}:`, err);
            }
        }

        if (base64Images.length === 0) {
            return c.json({ error: 'Failed to load any images' }, 500);
        }

        console.log(`Sending ${base64Images.length} images to Claude for parsing...`);

        // Parse with Claude using base64 images
        // Use batch processing for large documents (> 10 pages) to avoid token limits
        const claudeParser = new ClaudePdfParser(apiKey);
        const BATCH_SIZE = 10; // Process 10 pages at a time
        const parsedData = base64Images.length > BATCH_SIZE
            ? await claudeParser.parseInBatches(base64Images, id, BATCH_SIZE, customPrompt)
            : await claudeParser.parseBase64Images(base64Images, id, customPrompt);

        // Store parsed data in document metadata
        const { error: updateError } = await supabase
            .from('project_documents')
            .update({
                metadata: {
                    ...document.metadata,
                    parsed_data: parsedData,
                    parsed_at: new Date().toISOString()
                }
            })
            .eq('id', id);

        if (updateError) {
            console.error('Failed to save parsed data:', updateError);
        }

        return c.json({
            success: true,
            document_id: id,
            parsed: parsedData,
            summary: {
                units_count: parsedData.units?.length ?? 0,
                total_baseboard_ft: parsedData.total_baseboard_ft ?? 0,
                trades: parsedData.summary?.trades ?? []
            }
        });
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Error parsing document with Claude:', error);
        return c.json({
            error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to parse document'
        }, 500);
    }
});

// GENERATE WORK ORDERS FROM PARSED DATA
documentsRoute.post('/documents/:id/generate-work-orders', async (c) => {

        return c.json({
            error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to generate work orders'
        }, 500);
    }
});

 * Doc-Parser HTTP Client
 *
 * Shared service for communicating with the doc-parser backend.
 * Used by both docling and hybrid engine proxy routes.
 */

const DOC_PARSER_URL = process.env.DOC_PARSER_URL || 'http://localhost:5000';

export interface DocParserJobResponse {
  job_id: string;
  status: 'queued' | 'processing' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'timeout';
  engine: 'docling' | 'hybrid';
  file: string;
}

export interface DocParserProgress {
  current_phase: string;
  phases: string[];
  current_page: number;
  total_pages: number;
  current_step: string;
  floorplans_found: number;
  tables_found: number;
  percent_complete: number;
}

export interface DocParserStatus {
  job_id: string;
  status: 'queued' | 'processing' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'timeout';
  engine: string;
  file_name: string;
  progress: DocParserProgress;
  error?: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
}

export interface DocParserResultSummary {
  pages_count: number;
  floorplans_count: number;
  tables_count: number;
  units_count: number;
  has_footer: boolean;
  total_doors: number;
  total_windows: number;
  total_baseboard_ft: number;
  trades: string[];
}

export interface DocParserConfidence {
  overall: number;
  room_dimensions: boolean;
  door_specifications: boolean;
  window_specifications: boolean;
  fixture_completeness: boolean;
  cross_page_synthesis: boolean;
  baseboard_data: boolean;
  unit_identification: boolean;
  per_page: Record<string, any>;
}

export interface DocParserUnit {
  unit_number: string;
  unit_type: string;
  floor: number;
  bedrooms: number;
  bathrooms: number;
  half_baths: number;
  area_sf: number;
  rooms: any[];
  doors: any[];
  windows: any[];
  fixtures: any[];
  upgrades: any[];
  general_notes: string[];
  source_page: number;
}

export interface DocParserResult {
  engine: string;
  pages: Record<number, any>;
  units: DocParserUnit[];
  tables: any[];
  footer_sample: any;
  general_notes: string[];
  synthesis: any;
  confidence: DocParserConfidence;
  summary: DocParserResultSummary;
}

/**
 * Submit a PDF for parsing with the specified engine.
 */
export async function submitParseJob(
  pdfBuffer: Buffer | ArrayBuffer,
  fileName: string,
  engine: 'docling' | 'hybrid',
  options: {
    provider?: string;
    tenantName?: string;
    projectName?: string;
    supabaseUrl?: string;
    supabaseKey?: string;
  } = {},
): Promise<DocParserJobResponse> {
  const endpoint = engine === 'hybrid' ? '/parse/hybrid' : '/parse/docling';

  const formData = new FormData();
  const blob = new Blob([new Uint8Array(pdfBuffer as any)], { type: 'application/pdf' });
  formData.append('file', blob, fileName);

  if (options.provider) {
    formData.append('provider', options.provider);
  }
  if (options.tenantName) {
    formData.append('tenant_name', options.tenantName);
  }
  if (options.projectName) {
    formData.append('project_name', options.projectName);
  }
  if (options.supabaseUrl) {
    formData.append('supabase_url', options.supabaseUrl);
  }
  if (options.supabaseKey) {
    formData.append('supabase_key', options.supabaseKey);
  }

  const response = await fetch(`${DOC_PARSER_URL}${endpoint}`, {
    method: 'POST',
    body: formData,
  });

  if (!response.ok) {
    const errorText = await response.text();
    throw new Error(
      `Doc-parser submission failed (${response.status}): ${errorText}`,
    );
  }

  return response.json();
}

/**
 * Get lightweight job status (poll-safe).
 * NEVER includes result data.
 */
export async function getJobStatus(
  jobId: string,
): Promise<DocParserStatus> {
  const response = await fetch(`${DOC_PARSER_URL}/status/${jobId}`);

  if (!response.ok) {
    if (response.status === 404) {
      throw new Error(`Job ${jobId} not found`);
    }
    throw new Error(`Status check failed (${response.status})`);
  }

  return response.json();
}

/**
 * Get full extraction results (fetch-once after completed).
 * Heavy payload - only call when status confirms "completed".
 */
export async function getJobResults(
  jobId: string,
  summaryOnly: boolean = false,
): Promise<{ job_id: string; status: string; result?: DocParserResult; summary?: DocParserResultSummary }> {
  const url = summaryOnly
    ? `${DOC_PARSER_URL}/results/${jobId}?summary=true`
    : `${DOC_PARSER_URL}/results/${jobId}`;

  const response = await fetch(url);

  if (!response.ok) {
    if (response.status === 404) {
      throw new Error(`Results for job ${jobId} not found`);
    }
    throw new Error(`Results fetch failed (${response.status})`);
  }

  return response.json();
}

/**
 * Check if doc-parser service is healthy and available.
 */
export async function checkHealth(): Promise<{
  status: string;
  engines: string[];
  dependencies: Record<string, boolean>;
}> {
  try {
    const response = await fetch(`${DOC_PARSER_URL}/health`, {
      signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5000),
    });
    if (!response.ok) {
      return {
        status: 'unhealthy',
        engines: [],
        dependencies: {},
      };
    }
    return response.json();
  } catch {
    return {
      status: 'unreachable',
      engines: [],
      dependencies: {},
    };
  }
}

/**
 * Map hybrid engine output to ParsedDocument-compatible format.
 *
 * When engine is "hybrid", the units array is already
 * ParsedDocument-compatible. This mapper normalizes the
 * response for the frontend.
 */
export function mapToParseDocument(
  result: DocParserResult,
  documentId: string,
): {
  document_id: string;
  units: DocParserUnit[];
  general_notes: string[];
  total_baseboard_ft: number;
  summary: DocParserResultSummary;
  confidence: DocParserConfidence;
} {
  return {
    document_id: documentId,
    units: result.units || [],
    general_notes: result.general_notes || [],
    total_baseboard_ft: result.summary?.total_baseboard_ft || 0,
    summary: result.summary,
    confidence: result.confidence,
  };
}

---
description: Parse Architectural PDFs into structured JSON and mock Supabase storage
---
# Document Parser Workflow

This workflow processes architectural PDF documents to extract floorplans, tables, and site photos, perform AI-based structural analysis, and compress the outputs into a mock Supabase storage directory.

## Steps

1. **Start the Docker Container**
   Ensure the `doc-parser` service is running in Docker.
// turbo
   `cd doc-parser && docker-compose up -d --build doc-parser`

2. **Run the Processing Pipeline**
   Execute the `process_document.py` script and pass the path to the PDF, along with the tenant and project names. This triggers the background docling task, waits for completion, and generates compressed JPEGs and JSON data.
// turbo
   `cd doc-parser && python process_document.py --pdf demo.pdf --tenant demo_tenant --project demo_project`

## Full Supabase Pipeline (parse_document_pipeline.py)

Use this when you want pages, images, and OCR results persisted to Supabase.

### Prerequisites
- Supabase running (`supabase start` on Windows)
- doc-parser container running (`docker compose up -d` in `doc-parser/` on Windows)
- Service role key from `supabase status` output
- Migration `20260312000001_document_pages_ocr.sql` applied (`supabase db reset` or `apply-migration.ps1`)

### Run
```
python doc-parser/parse_document_pipeline.py \
  --pdf path/to/file.pdf \
  --tenant my_tenant \
  --project my_project \
  --supabase-url http://host.docker.internal:54321 \
  --supabase-key <service_role_key>
```

### What it writes to Supabase
| Table | Content |
|---|---|
| `project_documents` | One row — PDF metadata + storage URL |
| `document_pages` | One row per page — category, storage_url, thumbnail_url |
| `document_images` | One row per extracted image (floorplan/site_photo/diagram) |
| `ocr_results` | One row per table — markdown + structured_data JSON |

### Notes
- `server.py` must be rebuilt in Docker after any changes: `docker compose up -d --build`
- Raw results JSON saved alongside PDF as `<name>_pipeline_results.json` for debugging
- `PROGRAM_STATUS=develop_without_ai` in `.env` skips LLM calls (mocked floorplan data)

    "code": "routes_file = '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/apps/api/src/routes/doc-parser-routes.ts'\n\ncontent = '''/**\n * Doc-Parser Proxy Routes for Hono API\n *\n * Integrates doc-parser service into the existing work-orders API.\n * Provides engine selection, status polling, result retrieval,\n * and a full end-to-end pipeline with Supabase DB persistence.\n *\n * Routes:\n *   POST /api/doc-parser/parse           - Parse document (engine selection, returns job_id)\n *   POST /api/doc-parser/pipeline        - Full pipeline: submit + poll + persist to DB\n *   GET  /api/doc-parser/status/:jobId  - Lightweight status (poll-safe)\n *   GET  /api/doc-parser/results/:jobId - Full results (fetch-once)\n *   GET  /api/doc-parser/health         - Service health check\n */\n\nimport { Hono } from \\'hono\\';\nimport { createClient } from \\'@supabase/supabase-js\\';\nimport { SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY } from \\'@repo/config\\';\nimport { getScopedClient } from \\'../middleware/supabase\\';\nimport {\n  submitParseJob,\n  getJobStatus,\n  getJobResults,\n  checkHealth,\n  mapToParseDocument,\n  type DocParserJobResponse,\n  type DocParserStatus,\n  type DocParserResult,\n} from \\'../services/doc-parser-client\\';\n\nconst docParserRoutes = new Hono();\n\n// ============================================================\n// POST /parse\n// Accepts JSON { document_id, engine }\n// Fetches PDF from Supabase storage, forwards to doc-parser\n// Returns job_id immediately — caller must poll /status/:jobId\n// ============================================================\n\ndocParserRoutes.post(\\'/parse\\', async (c) => {\n  const body = await c.req.json().catch(() => ({}));\n  const documentId = body?.document_id;\n  const engine = (body?.engine || \\'hybrid\\') as string;\n\n  if (!documentId) {\n    return c.json({ error: \\'document_id is required\\' }, 400);\n  }\n\n  if (engine === \\'claude-direct\\') {\n    return c.json({\n      error: \\'claude-direct engine should be handled by existing route handler\\',\n      hint: \\'This route only handles docling and hybrid engines\\',\n    }, 400);\n  }\n\n  if (engine !== \\'docling\\' && engine !== \\'hybrid\\') {\n    return c.json(\n      { error: `Unknown engine: ${engine}. Use \\'claude-direct\\', \\'docling\\', or \\'hybrid\\'.` },\n      400,\n    );\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);\n    const { data: document, error: fetchError } = await supabase\n      .from(\\'project_documents\\')\n      .select(\\'*\\')\n      .eq(\\'id\\', documentId)\n      .single();\n\n    if (fetchError || !document) {\n      return c.json({ error: \\'Document not found\\' }, 404);\n    }\n    if (!document.file_type?.includes(\\'pdf\\')) {\n      return c.json({ error: \\'Document is not a PDF\\' }, 400);\n    }\n\n    const { data: fileData, error: downloadError } = await supabase.storage\n      .from(\\'project-documents\\')\n      .download(document.file_url);\n\n    if (downloadError || !fileData) {\n      console.error(\\'[doc-parser] Storage download failed:\\', downloadError);\n      return c.json({ error: \\'Failed to download document from storage\\' }, 500);\n    }\n\n    const pdfBuffer = await fileData.arrayBuffer();\n\n    const jobResponse = await submitParseJob(\n      pdfBuffer,\n      document.title || `document-${documentId}.pdf`,\n      engine as \\'docling\\' | \\'hybrid\\',\n      {\n        provider: body?.provider || process.env.DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER,\n        tenantName: body?.tenant_name,\n        projectName: body?.project_name || documentId,\n        supabaseUrl: process.env.SUPABASE_URL,\n        supabaseKey: process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY,\n      },\n    );\n\n    return c.json({\n      engine,\n      job_id: jobResponse.job_id,\n      status: jobResponse.status,\n      poll_url: `/api/doc-parser/status/${jobResponse.job_id}`,\n      results_url: `/api/doc-parser/results/${jobResponse.job_id}`,\n    });\n  } catch (error) {\n    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : \\'Unknown error\\';\n    console.error(`[doc-parser] Parse submission failed: ${message}`);\n    return c.json({ error: `Doc-parser submission failed: ${message}` }, 502);\n  }\n});\n\n// ============================================================\n// POST /pipeline\n// Full end-to-end pipeline — blocks until complete:\n//   1. Download PDF from Supabase storage\n//   2. Submit to doc-parser\n//   3. Poll internally until completed / failed / timeout\n//   4. Persist document_pages + ocr_results to DB\n//   5. Return summary\n//\n// Body: {\n//   document_id: string        (required)\n//   engine?: \\'docling\\'|\\'hybrid\\'  (default: \\'hybrid\\')\n//   poll_interval_ms?: number  (default: 3000)\n//   timeout_ms?: number        (default: 600000 = 10 min)\n//   tenant_name?: string\n//   project_name?: string\n// }\n//\n// Returns: {\n//   document_id, job_id, engine, status,\n//   pages_persisted, ocr_tables_persisted, summary,\n//   poll_url, results_url\n// }\n//\n// Note: Uses SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY for DB writes (bypasses RLS).\n// ============================================================\n\ndocParserRoutes.post(\\'/pipeline\\', async (c) => {\n  const body = await c.req.json().catch(() => ({}));\n  const documentId = body?.document_id as string | undefined;\n  const engine = (body?.engine || \\'hybrid\\') as \\'docling\\' | \\'hybrid\\';\n  const pollIntervalMs = Number(body?.poll_interval_ms ?? 3000);\n  const timeoutMs = Number(body?.timeout_ms ?? 600_000);\n\n  if (!documentId) {\n    return c.json({ error: \\'document_id is required\\' }, 400);\n  }\n  if (engine !== \\'docling\\' && engine !== \\'hybrid\\') {\n    return c.json(\n      { error: `Unknown engine: ${engine}. Use \\'docling\\' or \\'hybrid\\'.` },\n      400,\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Service-role client for DB writes (bypasses RLS)\n  const serviceClient = createClient(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY);\n\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  // 1. Fetch document metadata\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  const { data: document, error: fetchError } = await serviceClient\n    .from(\\'project_documents\\')\n    .select(\\'*\\')\n    .eq(\\'id\\', documentId)\n    .single();\n\n  if (fetchError || !document) {\n    return c.json({ error: \\'Document not found\\' }, 404);\n  }\n  if (!document.file_type?.includes(\\'pdf\\')) {\n    return c.json({ error: \\'Document is not a PDF\\' }, 400);\n  }\n\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  // 2. Download PDF from storage\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  const { data: fileData, error: downloadError } = await serviceClient.storage\n    .from(\\'project-documents\\')\n    .download(document.file_url);\n\n  if (downloadError || !fileData) {\n    console.error(\\'[pipeline] Storage download failed:\\', downloadError);\n    return c.json({ error: \\'Failed to download document from storage\\' }, 500);\n  }\n\n  const pdfBuffer = await fileData.arrayBuffer();\n\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  // 3. Submit to doc-parser\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  let jobResponse: DocParserJobResponse;\n  try {\n    jobResponse = await submitParseJob(\n      pdfBuffer,\n      document.title || `document-${documentId}.pdf`,\n      engine,\n      {\n        supabaseUrl: process.env.SUPABASE_URL,\n        supabaseKey: process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY,\n        projectName: body?.project_name || documentId,\n        tenantName: body?.tenant_name,\n      },\n    );\n  } catch (err) {\n    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \\'Unknown error\\';\n    console.error(\\'[pipeline] Job submission failed:\\', msg);\n    return c.json({ error: `Doc-parser submission failed: ${msg}` }, 502);\n  }\n\n  const jobId = jobResponse.job_id;\n  console.log(`[pipeline] Job ${jobId} submitted (engine=${engine})`);\n\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  // 4. Poll until complete or timeout\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\n  let status: DocParserStatus;\n\n  while (true) {\n    if (Date.now() > deadline) {\n      return c.json({\n        error: `Pipeline timed out after ${timeoutMs / 1000}s`,\n        job_id: jobId,\n      }, 504);\n    }\n\n    try {\n      status = await getJobStatus(jobId);\n    } catch (err) {\n      const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \\'Unknown error\\';\n      return c.json({ error: `Status poll failed: ${msg}`, job_id: jobId }, 502);\n    }\n\n    console.log(\n      `[pipeline] Job ${jobId} status=${status.status}` +\n      ` (${status.progress?.percent_complete ?? 0}%)`,\n    );\n\n    if (status.status === \\'completed\\') break;\n    if (status.status === \\'failed\\' || status.status === \\'timeout\\') {\n      return c.json({\n        error: `Doc-parser job ${status.status}: ${status.error ?? \\'no details\\'}`,\n        job_id: jobId,\n      }, 502);\n    }\n\n    await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, pollIntervalMs));\n  }\n\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  // 5. Fetch full results\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  let rawResults: { job_id: string; status: string; result?: DocParserResult };\n  try {\n    rawResults = await getJobResults(jobId, false);\n  } catch (err) {\n    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \\'Unknown error\\';\n    return c.json({ error: `Results fetch failed: ${msg}`, job_id: jobId }, 502);\n  }\n\n  const result = rawResults.result;\n  if (!result) {\n    return c.json(\n      { error: \\'Job completed but result payload is empty\\', job_id: jobId },\n      502,\n    );\n  }\n\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  // 6. Persist document_pages\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  const pageEntries = Object.entries(result.pages || {});\n  const pageIdMap: Record<number, string> = {};\n\n  for (const [pageNumStr, pageData] of pageEntries) {\n    const pageNumber = parseInt(pageNumStr, 10);\n    const pd = pageData as any;\n\n    let category: string = \\'text\\';\n    if (pd?.has_floorplan || pd?.is_floorplan)        category = \\'floorplan\\';\n    else if (pd?.has_table || (pd?.table_count ?? 0) > 0) category = \\'table\\';\n    else if (pd?.is_site_photo)                        category = \\'site_photo\\';\n    else if (pd?.is_diagram)                           category = \\'diagram\\';\n    else if (pd?.has_text && (pd?.has_floorplan || pd?.has_table)) category = \\'mixed\\';\n\n    const { data: pageRow, error: pageErr } = await serviceClient\n      .from(\\'document_pages\\')\n      .upsert(\n        {\n          document_id: documentId,\n          page_number: pageNumber,\n          storage_url: pd?.storage_url ?? pd?.image_url ?? null,\n          thumbnail_url: pd?.thumbnail_url ?? null,\n          category,\n          raw_text: pd?.raw_text ?? pd?.text ?? null,\n          metadata: pd ?? null,\n        },\n        { onConflict: \\'document_id,page_number\\', ignoreDuplicates: false },\n      )\n      .select(\\'id\\')\n      .single();\n\n    if (pageErr) {\n      console.error(\n        `[pipeline] document_pages upsert failed for page ${pageNumber}:`,\n        pageErr.message,\n      );\n    } else if (pageRow) {\n      pageIdMap[pageNumber] = pageRow.id;\n    }\n  }\n\n  console.log(\n    `[pipeline] Persisted ${Object.keys(pageIdMap).length}/${pageEntries.length} pages`,\n  );\n\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  // 7. Persist ocr_results (one row per extracted table)\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  const tables: any[] = result.tables || [];\n  let ocrCount = 0;\n\n  for (let tableIndex = 0; tableIndex < tables.length; tableIndex++) {\n    const tbl = tables[tableIndex] as any;\n    const pageNumber: number | null = tbl?.page_number ?? tbl?.source_page ?? null;\n    const pageId = pageNumber !== null ? (pageIdMap[pageNumber] ?? null) : null;\n\n    const { error: ocrErr } = await serviceClient\n      .from(\\'ocr_results\\')\n      .insert({\n        document_id: documentId,\n        page_id: pageId,\n        table_index: tableIndex,\n        markdown: tbl?.markdown ?? tbl?.content ?? null,\n        structured_data: tbl?.structured_data ?? tbl?.data ?? tbl ?? null,\n        confidence: tbl?.confidence ?? null,\n      });\n\n    if (ocrErr) {\n      console.error(\n        `[pipeline] ocr_results insert failed for table ${tableIndex}:`,\n        ocrErr.message,\n      );\n    } else {\n      ocrCount++;\n    }\n  }\n\n  console.log(`[pipeline] Persisted ${ocrCount}/${tables.length} OCR table rows`);\n\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  // 8. Return summary\n  // ----------------------------------------------------------\n  return c.json({\n    document_id: documentId,\n    job_id: jobId,\n    engine,\n    status: \\'completed\\',\n    pages_persisted: Object.keys(pageIdMap).length,\n    ocr_tables_persisted: ocrCount,\n    summary: result.summary ?? null,\n    poll_url: `/api/doc-parser/status/${jobId}`,\n    results_url: `/api/doc-parser/results/${jobId}`,\n  });\n});\n\n// ============================================================\n// GET /api/doc-parser/status/:jobId\n// Lightweight status for polling (poll-safe, small payload)\n// Safe to call every 2-5 seconds.\n// ============================================================\n\ndocParserRoutes.get(\\'/status/:jobId\\', async (c) => {\n  const jobId = c.req.param(\\'jobId\\');\n\n  try {\n    const status: DocParserStatus = await getJobStatus(jobId);\n    const safeStatus = { ...status };\n    delete (safeStatus as any).result;\n    return c.json(safeStatus);\n  } catch (error) {\n    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : \\'Unknown error\\';\n    if (message.includes(\\'not found\\')) {\n      return c.json({ error: `Job ${jobId} not found` }, 404);\n    }\n    return c.json({ error: `Status check failed: ${message}` }, 502);\n  }\n});\n\n// ============================================================\n// GET /api/doc-parser/results/:jobId\n// Full results — fetch once after status confirms \"completed\".\n// Heavy payload. Includes ParsedDocument-compatible mapping.\n// ============================================================\n\ndocParserRoutes.get(\\'/results/:jobId\\', async (c) => {\n  const jobId = c.req.param(\\'jobId\\');\n  const summaryOnly = c.req.query(\\'summary\\') === \\'true\\';\n\n  try {\n    const data = await getJobResults(jobId, summaryOnly);\n\n    if (summaryOnly) {\n      return c.json(data);\n    }\n\n    const result = data.result as DocParserResult | undefined;\n    if (result?.units) {\n      return c.json({\n        ...data,\n        parsed: mapToParseDocument(result, jobId),\n      });\n    }\n\n    return c.json(data);\n  } catch (error) {\n    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : \\'Unknown error\\';\n    if (message.includes(\\'not found\\')) {\n      return c.json({ error: `Results for job ${jobId} not found` }, 404);\n    }\n    return c.json({ error: `Results fetch failed: ${message}` }, 502);\n  }\n});\n\n// ============================================================\n// GET /api/doc-parser/health\n// Service health check with dependency status\n// ============================================================\n\ndocParserRoutes.get(\\'/health\\', async (c) => {\n  const health = await checkHealth();\n  const statusCode = health.status === \\'healthy\\' ? 200 : 503;\n  return c.json(health, statusCode);\n});\n\nexport { docParserRoutes };\nexport default docParserRoutes;\n'''\n\nwith open(routes_file, 'w') as f:\n    f.write(content)\n\nprint(f'Written {len(content)} chars to {routes_file}')\n# Verify key sections present\nfor marker in ['POST /pipeline', 'createClient', 'SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY', 'document_pages', 'ocr_results', 'pages_persisted']:\n    assert marker in content, f'MISSING: {marker}'\n    print(f'  OK: {marker}')\n"

```

### Endpoints

| Method | Path               | Description                            |
|--------|--------------------|----------------------------------------|
| POST   | `/parse/docling`   | Docling-only engine (primary)          |
| POST   | `/parse/hybrid`    | Hybrid: Docling decompose + Claude extract |
| GET    | `/status/{job_id}` | Poll for progress (safe to call every 2-5s) |
| GET    | `/results/{job_id}`| Fetch full results once complete       |
| GET    | `/health`          | Dependency status check                |
| GET    | `/jobs`            | List all jobs                          |

### Pipeline (Docling engine)
1. **Docling** — extracts text, tables, images from PDF
2. **Classification** — Tesseract + color variance separates floorplans / site photos / diagrams
3. **LLM analysis** — Claude 3.5 Sonnet (via OpenRouter) extracts rooms, doors, windows, areas
4. **Cross-page synthesis** — second LLM call consolidates multi-page results
5. **Confidence scoring** — quality score attached to result

### LLM Provider
Default: `anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet` via OpenRouter (LiteLLM under the hood).
Override with `DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER` env var.

---

## Starting the Stack

```powershell
# Full reset + restart (kills zombies, resets DB, seeds, starts dev servers)
powershell -File scripts/reset-and-start.ps1

# Or manually:
npx supabase start
pnpm dev

# Doc-parser (separate):
cd doc-parser && docker compose up -d
```

```powershell
# Kill zombie Node processes on ports 3000-3005
powershell -File scripts/kill-dev-servers.ps1

# Nuclear option
taskkill /F /IM node.exe
```

description: Parse Architectural PDFs into structured JSON and persist to Supabase
---
# Document Parser Workflow

This workflow processes architectural PDF documents to extract floorplans, tables, and site photos,
perform AI-based structural analysis, and persist results to Supabase.

Two approaches are available — choose based on your context:

| Approach | Best for | Blocks caller? |
|---|---|---|
| **HTTP Pipeline** (`POST /api/doc-parser/pipeline`) | Other AIs, frontends, automation | Yes (polls internally) |
| **Python CLI** (`parse_document_pipeline.py`) | Local dev, debugging, scripting | Yes |
| **HTTP Proxy** (`POST /api/doc-parser/parse`) | Custom polling loops | No (returns job_id) |

---

## Prerequisites (all approaches)

- Supabase running — `supabase start` on Windows
- doc-parser container running — `docker compose up -d` in `doc-parser/` on Windows
- Migration `20260312000001_document_pages_ocr.sql` applied
- PDF already uploaded to `project-documents` storage bucket and recorded in `project_documents` table

---

## Approach 1 — HTTP Pipeline Endpoint (recommended for AI agents)

The Hono API exposes a single blocking endpoint that handles the full pipeline internally:
submit → poll → persist → return summary.

### Endpoint
```
POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline
Content-Type: application/json
```

### Request body
```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid from project_documents>",
  "engine": "hybrid",
  "poll_interval_ms": 3000,
  "timeout_ms": 600000
}
```

| Field | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `document_id` | Yes | — | UUID from `project_documents` table |
| `engine` | No | `hybrid` | `docling` or `hybrid` |
| `poll_interval_ms` | No | `3000` | How often to poll doc-parser internally |
| `timeout_ms` | No | `600000` | 10 min max wait before 504 |
| `tenant_name` | No | — | Passed to doc-parser for storage namespacing |
| `project_name` | No | `document_id` | Passed to doc-parser |

### Response
```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid>",
  "job_id": "<doc-parser job id>",
  "engine": "hybrid",
  "status": "completed",
  "pages_persisted": 12,
  "ocr_tables_persisted": 4,
  "summary": {
    "pages_count": 12,
    "floorplans_count": 3,
    "tables_count": 4,
    "units_count": 6
  },
  "poll_url": "/api/doc-parser/status/<job_id>",
  "results_url": "/api/doc-parser/results/<job_id>"
}
```

### curl example
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"document_id": "<uuid>", "engine": "hybrid"}'
```

### What it writes to Supabase
| Table | Content |
|---|---|
| `document_pages` | One row per page — category, storage_url, thumbnail_url, raw_text, metadata |
| `ocr_results` | One row per extracted table — markdown + structured_data JSON |

> Uses `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` for DB writes (bypasses RLS).
> The PDF must already exist in `project_documents` — upload it first via
> `POST /work-orders/projects/:projectId/documents`.

---

## Approach 2 — HTTP Proxy (custom polling)

Returns a `job_id` immediately. Caller is responsible for polling and DB persistence.

```bash
# 1. Submit
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/parse \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"document_id": "<uuid>", "engine": "hybrid"}'
# -> { "job_id": "...", "poll_url": "..." }

# 2. Poll
curl http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/status/<job_id>
# -> { "status": "processing", "progress": { "percent_complete": 45 } }

# 3. Fetch results
curl http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/results/<job_id>
```

---

## Approach 3 — Python CLI Pipeline

Use when running locally or when you need direct control over the pipeline.

### Run
```bash
python doc-parser/parse_document_pipeline.py \
  --pdf path/to/file.pdf \
  --tenant my_tenant \
  --project my_project \
  --supabase-url http://host.docker.internal:54321 \
  --supabase-key <service_role_key>
```

### What it writes to Supabase
| Table | Content |
|---|---|
| `project_documents` | One row — PDF metadata + storage URL |
| `document_pages` | One row per page — category, storage_url, thumbnail_url |
| `document_images` | One row per extracted image (floorplan/site_photo/diagram) |
| `ocr_results` | One row per table — markdown + structured_data JSON |

### Notes
- `server.py` must be rebuilt in Docker after any changes: `docker compose up -d --build`
- Raw results JSON saved alongside PDF as `<name>_pipeline_results.json` for debugging
- `PROGRAM_STATUS=develop_without_ai` in `.env` skips LLM calls (mocked data)

---

## Starting the doc-parser container (Windows)

```powershell
cd C:\Users\3900X\Documents\GitHub\agentZero\voicelog-scaffold\doc-parser
docker compose up -d
# or rebuild after server.py changes:
docker compose up -d --build
```

## Health check
```bash
curl http://localhost:5000/health
curl http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/health
```

### 1b. Single-page pipeline

Extract page N from the PDF using `pdf-lib`, parse only that page, upsert into `document_pages`.
Useful for re-processing a specific page, previewing, or targeted extraction.

> **Requires:** `pdf-lib` installed \u2014 run `pnpm install` on Windows after adding it to `apps/api/package.json`.

```

Content-Type: application/json
```

```json
{

  "timeout_ms": 120000
}

|---|---|---|---|
| `document_id` | Yes | \u2014 | UUID from `project_documents` table |
| `page_number` | Yes | \u2014 | 1-indexed page number |
| `engine` | No | `hybrid` | `docling` or `hybrid` |
| `poll_interval_ms` | No | `3000` | Internal poll interval |
| `timeout_ms` | No | `120000` | 2 min default (single page is fast) |

```bash

  "page_id": "<document_pages row uuid>",
  "status": "completed",
  "ocr_tables_persisted": 1,
  "summary": { ... },
  "poll_url": "/api/doc-parser/status/<job_id>",
  "results_url": "/api/doc-parser/results/<job_id>"
}
```

---

### 1c. Idempotent reprocess

Re-run the full pipeline for a document that has already been parsed.
Safe to call multiple times \u2014 uses upsert on `document_id,page_number`.
Useful when doc-parser is updated or results need refreshing.

```
POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess
Content-Type: application/json
```

Same body as `/pipeline`. Response is identical plus `"reprocessed": true`.

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess \\
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \\
  -d \'{"document_id": "<uuid>", "engine": "hybrid"}\'
```

---

### What pipeline endpoints write to Supabase

|---|---|
| `document_pages` | One row per page \u2014 category, storage_url, thumbnail_url, raw_text, metadata |
| `ocr_results` | One row per extracted table \u2014 markdown + structured_data JSON |

---
description: Parse Architectural PDFs into structured JSON and persist to Supabase
---
# Document Parser Workflow

This workflow processes architectural PDF documents to extract floorplans, tables, and site photos,
perform AI-based structural analysis, and persist results to Supabase.

| Approach | Best for | Blocks caller? |
|---|---|---|
| **HTTP Pipeline** (`POST /api/doc-parser/pipeline`) | Full document, AI agents, automation | Yes |
| **HTTP Single-Page** (`POST /api/doc-parser/pipeline/page`) | One page, re-processing, previewing | Yes |
| **HTTP Reprocess** (`POST /api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess`) | Idempotent re-run after parser updates | Yes |
| **HTTP Read** (`GET /api/doc-parser/documents/...`) | Browse already-parsed results | No (DB only) |
| **HTTP Proxy** (`POST /api/doc-parser/parse`) | Custom polling loops | No (returns job_id) |
| **Python CLI** (`parse_document_pipeline.py`) | Local dev, debugging, scripting | Yes |

---

## Prerequisites (all approaches)

- Supabase running — `supabase start` on Windows
- doc-parser container running — `docker compose up -d` in `doc-parser/` on Windows
- Migration `20260312000001_document_pages_ocr.sql` applied
- PDF already uploaded to `project-documents` storage bucket and recorded in `project_documents` table

---

## Approach 1 — Full Document Pipeline

Handles the full pipeline internally: submit → poll → persist → return summary. Blocks until complete.

```
POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline
Content-Type: application/json
```

```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid from project_documents>",
  "engine": "hybrid",
  "poll_interval_ms": 3000,
  "timeout_ms": 600000
}
```

| Field | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `document_id` | Yes | — | UUID from `project_documents` table |
| `engine` | No | `hybrid` | `docling` or `hybrid` |
| `poll_interval_ms` | No | `3000` | How often to poll doc-parser internally |
| `timeout_ms` | No | `600000` | 10 min max wait before 504 |
| `tenant_name` | No | — | Passed to doc-parser for storage namespacing |
| `project_name` | No | `document_id` | Passed to doc-parser |

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"document_id": "<uuid>", "engine": "hybrid"}'
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid>",
  "job_id": "<doc-parser job id>",
  "engine": "hybrid",
  "status": "completed",
  "pages_persisted": 12,
  "ocr_tables_persisted": 4,
  "summary": { "pages_count": 12, "floorplans_count": 3, "tables_count": 4 },
  "poll_url": "/api/doc-parser/status/<job_id>",
  "results_url": "/api/doc-parser/results/<job_id>"
}
```

---

## Approach 2 — Single-Page Pipeline

Extract page N from the PDF using `pdf-lib`, parse only that page, upsert into `document_pages`.
Useful for re-processing a specific page, previewing, or targeted extraction without re-running the whole document.

> **Requires:** `pdf-lib` — run `pnpm install` on Windows after the `apps/api/package.json` change.

```
POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline/page
Content-Type: application/json
```

```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid>",
  "page_number": 8,
  "engine": "hybrid",
  "timeout_ms": 120000
}
```

| Field | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `document_id` | Yes | — | UUID from `project_documents` table |
| `page_number` | Yes | — | **1-indexed** page number |
| `engine` | No | `hybrid` | `docling` or `hybrid` |
| `poll_interval_ms` | No | `3000` | Internal poll interval |
| `timeout_ms` | No | `120000` | 2 min default (single page is fast) |

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline/page \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"document_id": "<uuid>", "page_number": 8, "engine": "hybrid"}'
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid>",
  "job_id": "<doc-parser job id>",
  "engine": "hybrid",
  "page_number": 8,
  "page_id": "<document_pages row uuid>",
  "status": "completed",
  "ocr_tables_persisted": 1,
  "summary": { "pages_count": 1 },
  "poll_url": "/api/doc-parser/status/<job_id>",
  "results_url": "/api/doc-parser/results/<job_id>"
}
```

---

## Approach 3 — Idempotent Reprocess

Re-run the full pipeline for a document that has already been parsed.
Safe to call multiple times — uses upsert on `document_id,page_number`.
Useful when doc-parser is updated or results need refreshing.

```
POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess
Content-Type: application/json
```

Same body as `/pipeline`. Response is identical plus `"reprocessed": true`.

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"document_id": "<uuid>", "engine": "hybrid"}'
```

---

## Approach 4 — Read Endpoints (DB only, no doc-parser call)

Query already-parsed results directly from the database. No doc-parser involved.
Use these to browse results after any pipeline approach has run.

```bash
# List all pages for a document
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/pages

# Filter by category
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/pages?category=floorplan

# Paginate
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/pages?limit=20&offset=0

# Get a specific page by 1-indexed page number
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/pages/8

# List all OCR/table results
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/ocr

# Filter OCR by page
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/ocr?page_number=8
```

**Category values:** `floorplan` | `table` | `site_photo` | `diagram` | `mixed` | `text`

**Pages response:**
```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid>",
  "pages": [
    {
      "id": "<uuid>",
      "page_number": 1,
      "storage_url": "https://...",
      "thumbnail_url": "https://...",
      "category": "floorplan",
      "raw_text": "...",
      "created_at": "..."
    }
  ],
  "count": 12,
  "offset": 0,
  "limit": 100
}
```

**OCR response:**
```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid>",
  "ocr_results": [
    {
      "id": "<uuid>",
      "page_id": "<document_pages uuid>",
      "table_index": 0,
      "markdown": "| Col A | Col B |\n|---|---|\n| ... |",
      "structured_data": {},
      "confidence": 0.92
    }
  ],
  "count": 4
}
```

---

## Approach 5 — HTTP Proxy (custom polling)

Returns a `job_id` immediately. Caller is responsible for polling and DB persistence.

```bash
# 1. Submit
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/parse \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"document_id": "<uuid>", "engine": "hybrid"}'
# -> { "job_id": "...", "poll_url": "..." }

# 2. Poll
curl http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/status/<job_id>
# -> { "status": "processing", "progress": { "percent_complete": 45 } }

# 3. Fetch results
curl http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/results/<job_id>
```

---

## Approach 6 — Python CLI Pipeline

Use when running locally or when you need direct control over the pipeline.

### Run
```bash
python doc-parser/parse_document_pipeline.py \
  --pdf path/to/file.pdf \
  --tenant my_tenant \
  --project my_project \
  --supabase-url http://host.docker.internal:54321 \
  --supabase-key <service_role_key>
```

### What it writes to Supabase
| Table | Content |
|---|---|
| `project_documents` | One row — PDF metadata + storage URL |
| `document_pages` | One row per page — category, storage_url, thumbnail_url |
| `document_images` | One row per extracted image (floorplan/site_photo/diagram) |
| `ocr_results` | One row per table — markdown + structured_data JSON |

### Notes
- `server.py` must be rebuilt in Docker after any changes: `docker compose up -d --build`
- Raw results JSON saved alongside PDF as `<name>_pipeline_results.json` for debugging
- `PROGRAM_STATUS=develop_without_ai` in `.env` skips LLM calls (mocked data)

---

## What pipeline endpoints write to Supabase

| Table | Content |
|---|---|
| `document_pages` | One row per page — category, storage_url, thumbnail_url, raw_text, metadata |
| `ocr_results` | One row per extracted table — markdown + structured_data JSON |

> Uses `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` for DB writes (bypasses RLS).
> The PDF must already exist in `project_documents` — upload it first via
> `POST /work-orders/projects/:projectId/documents`.

    "text": "# Reliable File Writing Approach — Agent Zero\n\n## The Problem\nWriting large files with special characters (backslashes, quotes, unicode) fails via:\n- Terminal heredocs: shell interprets backslashes, breaks on `\\n`, `\\t`, `$var`\n- Python code_execution_tool with multiline strings: JSON escaping conflicts with string content\n- Raw strings `r'''...'''` in JSON: unicode escapes like `\\u2014` not processed\n\n## The Solution That Always Works\n\n### Pattern: Write script to /tmp, then execute\n\n```python\n# Step 1 — python runtime: write the script to /tmp\nscript = r'''\n# your actual file-writing code here\npath = '/target/file.md'\ncontent = \"\"\"...file content...\"\"\"\nwith open(path, 'w') as f:\n    f.write(content)\nprint(f'Written {len(content)} chars')\n'''\nwith open('/tmp/update_file.py', 'w') as f:\n    f.write(script)\nimport os\nos.system('python3 /tmp/update_file.py')\n```\n\n### Why it works\n- The outer python runtime writes the script as a raw string (no shell interpretation)\n- os.system() executes it in a clean subprocess\n- The inner script has full Python string handling with no JSON escaping conflicts\n\n### Alternative: Build content with string concatenation\nFor files with lots of special chars, build content line by line:\n```python\nlines = []\nlines.append('## Section')\nlines.append('| Col | Col |')\ncontent = chr(10).join(lines)\n```\n\n### Verification always\nAfter writing, always verify with:\n```bash\nwc -l /path/to/file && grep -n 'KeySection' /path/to/file\n```\n\n## What NOT to do\n- Do NOT use bash heredoc for files with backslashes or special chars\n- Do NOT put large multiline file content directly in code_execution_tool JSON args\n- Do NOT use f-strings with curly braces in content that also uses format strings"
  }
